- impeccable: Use when the user wants to design, redesign, shape, critique, audit, polish, clarify, distill, harden, optimize, adapt, animate, colorize, extract, or otherwise improve a frontend interface. Covers websites, landing pages, dashboards, product UI, app shells, components, forms, settings, onboarding, and empty states. Handles UX review, visual hierarchy, information architecture, cognitive load, accessibility, performance, responsive behavior, theming, anti-patterns, typography, fonts, spacing, layout, alignment, color, motion, micro-interactions, UX copy, error states, edge cases, i18n, and reusable design systems or tokens. Also use for bland designs that need to become bolder or more delightful, loud designs that should become quieter, live browser iteration on UI elements, or ambitious visual effects that should feel technically extraordinary. Not for backend-only or non-UI tasks.
- find-skills: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like "how do I do X", "find a skill for X", "is there a skill that can...", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill should be used when the user is looking for functionality that might exist as an installable skill.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request

file.content:
---
name: hChat feature roadmap (2026-05-09)
description: 6-phase plan agreed with heath to extend hChat with branching, multimodal, tools, MCP, encryption
type: project
originSessionId: 21f88703-b490-4493-82da-dd3c5a0b700e
---
Phased plan locked on 2026-05-09. Decisions:
- Branching: tree model (each message has parent_id + branch_index)
- Encryption: opt-in via config, off by default
- Per-conversation settings: denormalized (each conversation owns its own model/system/temp; presets are templates that *create* conversations)
- Tools: native first, user-configurable so the community can ship tool packs; MCP after that
- Compare mode: deferred indefinitely

Phases (one PR each):
1. Foundation polish — no schema/Message changes. Live token counter, slash commands, Ctrl+F, per-codeblock copy, reasoning collapse, empty-state suggestions, top_p/penalties, hover timestamps, streaming render perf.
2. Storage migration — schema_version table; conversations gets model/system_prompt/temperature/max_tokens/use_max_tokens/pinned/draft/parent_message_id; new presets table; messages gets parent_id/branch_index (pre-staged for phase 4). Auto-title, draft persistence, per-conversation settings, presets, pin.
3. Message content multimodal — Vec<ContentPart> instead of String. Image attachments + file drop.
4. Branching — wire up parent_id/branch_index from phase 2. Edit and regenerate create siblings instead of truncating.
5. Native tools (user-configurable, community-pack-able) → then MCP client.
6. Opt-in SQLCipher encryption + global hotkey/quick-ask window.

**Why:** Spreads risk — schema/Message-type changes are bundled into single migrations rather than dribbled across PRs. Each phase ships standalone.

**How to apply:** Start phase N+1 only after phase N's PR merges. If a phase grows beyond ~1500 LOC diff, split it.

3	description: How heath wants feature work delivered on hChat — PR cadence, clippy, review agents
4	type: feedback

6	---
7	For substantial feature work on hChat, deliver as one PR per logical phase (not one giant PR), and before opening each PR:
8	
9	1. Run `cargo build --release` and `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` and fix all warnings.
10	2. Spin up a review subagent (Agent tool, code-reviewer or general-purpose) to audit the diff before opening the PR.
11	3. Run `cargo test` if tests exist for what changed.
12	
13	**Why:** heath asked for "extremely thorough" testing on the hChat feature push (2026-05-09) and explicitly called out clippy + review agents. Skipping clippy on a Rust project is a known smell to him.
14	
15	**How to apply:** Any multi-feature push or anything touching `app.rs`/`api.rs`/`storage.rs`. For tiny one-line fixes, the full ritual is overkill.
16

---
name: hChat delivery standards
description: How heath wants feature work delivered on hChat — PR cadence, clippy, review agents
type: feedback
originSessionId: 21f88703-b490-4493-82da-dd3c5a0b700e
---
For substantial feature work on hChat, deliver as one PR per logical phase (not one giant PR), and before opening each PR:

1. Run `cargo build --release` and `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` and fix all warnings.
2. Spin up a review subagent (Agent tool, code-reviewer or general-purpose) to audit the diff before opening the PR.
3. Run `cargo test` if tests exist for what changed.

**Why:** heath asked for "extremely thorough" testing on the hChat feature push (2026-05-09) and explicitly called out clippy + review agents. Skipping clippy on a Rust project is a known smell to him.

**How to apply:** Any multi-feature push or anything touching `app.rs`/`api.rs`/`storage.rs`. For tiny one-line fixes, the full ritual is overkill.

All four jobs passed:
- ✅ `build-linux` — `hchat-linux-x86_64.tar.gz` + `.deb`
- ✅ `build-macos-arm64` — `hchat-macos-arm64.tar.gz` + `hChat.app.zip`

- ✅ `release` — GitHub Release created with auto-generated notes
- ✅ `update-homebrew` — formula pushed to your tap

Total time: ~7 minutes. Homebrew users can pull `0.6.0` with `brew update && brew upgrade hchat`.

One non-blocking warning across all jobs: Node.js 20 deprecation notice on the `actions/upload-artifact@v5` and friends. They're being force-run on Node 24 for now, but the actions themselves still target Node 20. Worth a small follow-up to bump those action versions when you're touching CI again — not urgent.

Phase 1 is officially shipped. Where to next?

- **Phase 3 (multimodal)** — most independent of what we just shipped, fastest payoff (image drop).
- **Phase 2 (storage)** — biggest leverage but biggest blast radius; do you want to live with `0.6.0` for a day first?

My same recommendation as before: live with `0.6.0` for a bit, start **Phase 3** when you're ready. Your call.

=== ASSISTANT ===
Honestly, that's the better call. Here's why I should have proposed it:

The schema migration in Phase 2 stores `messages.content TEXT`. If Phase 3 lands separately, we'd realize we need `content_json` (or a parts table) for multimodal and migrate *again*. Two migrations on user data when one would do — that's exactly the maintenance tail we just complained about with `model_limits`.

If we know multimodal is coming, the right move is to design the schema once: store `content` as JSON-serialized `Vec<ContentPart>` from day 1. Pure-text messages just become `[{"type":"text","text":"..."}]` — same shape OpenAI's chat completions API uses for multimodal anyway. Old text rows migrate by wrapping. Future-proof, one schema move.

So **2+3 bundled** lands as:

- **One migration** — schema_version table, per-conversation settings columns (model/system_prompt/temperature/max_tokens/use_max_tokens), pinned, draft, presets table, pre-staged `parent_message_id` + `branch_index` for Phase 4, `messages.content` as JSON parts
- **Phase 2 features** — auto-title via the model, draft persistence, per-conversation settings, presets library, pin
- **Phase 3 features** — `Message.content: Vec<ContentPart>`, drag-drop images for vision models, drag-drop text files inlined as fenced blocks

Estimated scope: ~2000–2500 LOC, one PR. Bigger than Phase 1 but coherent — every line traces back to the migration design.

What I'd **not** add to the bundle: Phase 4 (branching). It builds on Phase 2's pre-staged columns but the UI for sibling navigation is its own concern, and pulling it in pushes us past 3000 LOC. Better as the next PR right after — at which point we'd be 4 PRs from done (2+3, then 4, then 5, then 6).

If this scope sounds right, I'll plan it the same way Phase 1 went: design first, then I'll sketch the migration shape and key data-model decisions for you to ack before I write code. Sound good?

=== ASSISTANT ===
Yes, mostly. Caveats:

- **Same machine, terminal or desktop app**: Reopen Claude Code in `/Users/heath/code/hChat` and use `/resume` to pick this conversation, or `claude -c` for the most recent. Local conversation history lives in `~/.claude/projects/-Users-heath-code-hChat/` so any Claude Code interface on this machine can pick it up.
- **Memory I've saved persists** — your roadmap, delivery preferences, and the rest are in `~/.claude/projects/-Users-heath-code-hChat/memory/`. Future sessions will load them.
- **TodoWrite state, the active branch, and PR context all carry forward** because they're either in the conversation transcript or in git/GitHub.

What I'm **not** sure about: cross-machine continuation (e.g., closing your laptop and resuming on a separate desktop app on a different device). The transcript files are local; I don't know if Claude Code's desktop app syncs across machines or treats each install as separate. Worth testing if you actually need that.

The "remote control" / RemoteTrigger feature is something else — I haven't loaded its schema so I'd be guessing. If that's specifically what you meant, ping me when you're back and I'll find out properly.

Practical answer: when you're back, `cd ~/code/hChat && claude -c` from a terminal should drop you right back here. Have fun at the party.

content.text:
OK, I have the lay of the land. Here's the design sketch — please ack or push back before I start writing code.

---

# Phase 2+3 design sketch

## 1. Schema migration (single shot, idempotent)

Introduce `schema_version` table. Run all migrations on startup, in order, gated by `MAX(version)`.

```sql
-- v1: baseline (already exists in the wild)
-- v2: per-conversation settings + presets + branching pre-stage + JSON content

CREATE TABLE schema_version (version INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, applied_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')));

-- Conversations get all the settings that used to live globally on ChatApp.
ALTER TABLE conversations ADD COLUMN model TEXT;
ALTER TABLE conversations ADD COLUMN system_prompt TEXT;
ALTER TABLE conversations ADD COLUMN temperature REAL;
ALTER TABLE conversations ADD COLUMN max_tokens INTEGER;
ALTER TABLE conversations ADD COLUMN use_max_tokens INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0;  -- bool
ALTER TABLE conversations ADD COLUMN top_p REAL;
ALTER TABLE conversations ADD COLUMN frequency_penalty REAL;
ALTER TABLE conversations ADD COLUMN presence_penalty REAL;
ALTER TABLE conversations ADD COLUMN stop_sequences TEXT;  -- JSON array
ALTER TABLE conversations ADD COLUMN endpoint TEXT;        -- which endpoint this conv was created against
ALTER TABLE conversations ADD COLUMN pinned INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0;
ALTER TABLE conversations ADD COLUMN draft TEXT;           -- unsent input text

-- Messages: branching pre-stage + JSON parts.
ALTER TABLE messages ADD COLUMN parent_id INTEGER REFERENCES messages(id) ON DELETE CASCADE;
ALTER TABLE messages ADD COLUMN branch_index INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0;
ALTER TABLE messages ADD COLUMN created_at INTEGER;        -- epoch ms; null for pre-migration rows
-- `content` stays TEXT but its contract changes to JSON-serialized Vec<ContentPart>.
-- Migration step: wrap each existing row's content as [{"type":"text","text":"<old>"}].

CREATE TABLE presets (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    name TEXT NOT NULL,
    model TEXT,
    system_prompt TEXT,
    temperature REAL,
    max_tokens INTEGER,
    use_max_tokens INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    top_p REAL,
    frequency_penalty REAL,
    presence_penalty REAL,
    stop_sequences TEXT,
    endpoint TEXT,
    created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
);

CREATE INDEX idx_messages_parent ON messages(parent_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_conversations_pinned_updated ON conversations(pinned DESC, updated_at DESC);
```

**Why all in one migration**: a partial schema (e.g. settings columns but not `parent_id`) is a worse intermediate state than going straight to v2.

## 2. `ContentPart` enum (matches OpenAI wire format)

```rust
// src/message.rs
#[derive(Clone, Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]
#[serde(tag = "type", rename_all = "snake_case")]
pub enum ContentPart {
    Text { text: String },
    ImageUrl { image_url: ImageUrl },        // OpenAI: { url: "data:image/png;base64,...", detail?: "low"|"high" }
}

#[derive(Clone, Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct ImageUrl {
    pub url: String,                          // data URL or remote URL
    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    pub detail: Option<String>,
}

#[derive(Clone, Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct Message {
    pub role: Role,
    pub content: Vec<ContentPart>,
    #[serde(skip_serializing, default)]
    pub created_at: Option<i64>,
}
```

**Wire compatibility**: OpenAI accepts both `content: "string"` and `content: [parts...]`. We always send the array form. Local Ollama text-only models accept both. Vision-only providers are happy with the array form. ✅

**Helper API to keep app.rs sane** — most call sites just want concatenated text:

```rust
impl Message {
    pub fn text(role: Role, body: String) -> Self { /* wraps single Text part */ }
    pub fn text_str(&self) -> String { /* concatenate all Text parts; ignore images */ }
    pub fn append_text(&mut self, s: &str) { /* extend trailing Text or push new */ }
    pub fn is_empty_content(&self) -> bool { /* no parts, or only empty text */ }
    pub fn images(&self) -> impl Iterator<Item = &ImageUrl> { ... }
}
```

The ~30 `msg.content.push_str(...)` / `msg.content.len()` / `msg.content.clone()` sites in `app.rs` migrate to these helpers, mostly mechanically.

## 3. Per-conversation settings model

`ChatApp` keeps the current globals as **defaults for new conversations**. When a conversation is loaded, its settings override the globals into a `current_settings` struct that the UI mutates. On change, save to the conv row immediately (debounced via the existing save mechanism).

```rust
pub struct ConversationSettings {
    pub model: Option<String>,            // None = use global default
    pub system_prompt: String,
    pub temperature: f32,
    pub max_tokens: u32,
    pub use_max_tokens: bool,
    pub top_p: Option<f32>,
    pub frequency_penalty: Option<f32>,
    pub presence_penalty: Option<f32>,
    pub stop_sequences: Vec<String>,
    pub endpoint: Option<String>,
}
```

Settings panel shows per-conversation values with a "save as preset" button.

## 4. Presets

CRUD via a small modal (list / create / apply / delete). Applying a preset to the *current* conversation copies its values into `current_settings`. There's also a "New conversation from preset" entry point.

Default presets seeded on first migration (e.g., "Code review", "Brainstorm"). Heath, let me know if you want me to skip the seeding — easy to leave out.

## 5. Phase 3 features

- **Image attachments**: drag-drop image files onto the input area → become `ContentPart::ImageUrl { url: "data:image/...;base64,..." }`. Show thumbnails above the input. Send-only; no in-message image rendering yet (can add later if egui_extras image loading is reliable enough).
- **File drop**: drag-drop text files (.md, .rs, .py, .txt, .json, etc., size-capped at ~256KB) → inlined into the input as a fenced codeblock with the language inferred from extension.
- **Pin**: per-conversation toggle, sorted to top of sidebar.
- **Auto-title**: after the first assistant response in an untitled conversation, fire a small one-off completion (model = current; prompt = "Title this in ≤6 words: …") and update the row.
- **Draft persistence**: `input` text saved to `conversations.draft` on a debounce; restored on load.

## 6. What I'm explicitly **not** doing

- Branching UI / sibling navigation — Phase 4.
- Image rendering inside the message stream — defer; needs an async loader + placeholder.
- Per-message setting overrides — never agreed to that.
- MCP / tools — Phase 5.

## 7. Migration of existing user data

Forward-only. Wrap every `messages.content` row into `[{"type":"text","text":"<old>"}]` at v2 apply time, in a single transaction. If wrap fails on a row we abort the whole migration — the user keeps working on v1, error banner explains. (No realistic failure mode here, but worth being explicit.)

No down-migration. If they roll back the binary, v1 hChat reads `content` and sees JSON in the field — broken, but the data is recoverable. This matches the "one direction" approach common to CLI tools.

## 8. Test additions

- `tests/migration_v1_to_v2.rs` — open a v1-shaped DB, run init, verify schema_version=2 and content is wrapped.
- `tests/content_part_serde.rs` — round-trip pure-text and mixed text+image messages; assert wire format matches OpenAI examples.
- Unit tests on `Message::text_str()` / `append_text` / `is_empty_content`.

Ad-hoc manual test plan in PR description; I'll spin up a real Ollama (text) and OpenRouter (vision) check before requesting review.

## 9. Estimated diff

- `storage.rs`: ~+250 LOC (migration runner + new CRUD)
- `message.rs`: ~+120 LOC (parts + helpers)
- `api.rs`: ~+10 LOC (no real change — `Message` already serializes)
- `app.rs`: ~+400 LOC, ~−100 LOC (settings panel rework, preset modal, drag-drop handlers, draft hookup, pin sort, auto-title, ~30 content-access call sites updated)
- `config.rs`: minor (presets path, default preset toggle if any)
- new: `src/preset.rs` (~80 LOC)
- tests: ~+200 LOC

Rough total: **~2000–2200 LOC**. One PR.

## 10. PR ritual reminder

Per `feedback_delivery_standards.md`: build + clippy `-D warnings` + review subagent + tests, then PR.

---

**Decisions I need from you before I write a line:**

1. **Default endpoint per conversation** — store the endpoint URL on the conversation row? I added it above. Alternative: only store the model and assume current endpoint at chat time. Storing it is safer (a conv made against OpenRouter shouldn't silently re-route to localhost Ollama).
2. **Seed default presets?** — yes / no / let me see your starter set.
3. **Auto-title model** — same model as the conversation, or always fall back to a small/cheap one if the conv is using something expensive (e.g. opus)? Same-model is simpler; cheap-fallback respects bills.
4. **Anything in the "not doing" list you want pulled in?** — I'd prefer to keep scope tight.

Ack on the rest and I'll start with the migration + tests, then work outward.

Noted. That simplifies things meaningfully — I can drop the data-wrap step and the v1→v2 migration test entirely. New baseline plan:

- `init_tables()` creates the full v2 schema directly. No `ALTER`s, no row rewrites.
- I'll still add a tiny `schema_version` table — cheap insurance for *your* future changes (Phase 4 branching, Phase 6 SQLCipher), so you don't have to remember whether an ALTER already ran.
- Saves ~150 LOC and one test file from the estimate. New rough total: **~1850 LOC**.
- Practical impact for you: blow away `~/Library/Application Support/hchat/hchat.db` once before running the new build. I'll mention it in the PR description.

Three decisions still open before I start coding.

questions.question: Should each conversation remember which endpoint URL it was created against (not just the model name)?

options.label: Per-conversation endpoint (Recommended)
options.description: Conversation row stores endpoint URL. A conv started against OpenRouter won't silently re-route to localhost Ollama if you switch endpoints. Costs one extra TEXT column.

options.label: Global endpoint only
options.description: Only the model name is stored on the conv. Whatever endpoint is currently selected at chat time gets used. Simpler, but switching endpoints can quietly break old conversations whose model isn't available locally.

questions.question: Should I seed a few default presets on first launch?

options.description: Presets table starts empty; you build your own. Cleanest, no opinions baked in.

options.label: Seed 2-3 generic starters

questions.question: Which model should auto-title fire against?

options.label: Same model as the conversation (Recommended)
options.description: Simplest. One LLM call per new conv against whatever's selected. Cost is negligible (~6 tokens out) even on Opus.

options.label: Always a cheap fallback
options.description: Hard-code a cheap model (e.g. Haiku, or the first local Ollama model) for titling regardless of conv model. Saves cents on heavy-model conversations but adds config and a fallback path.

options.label: Skip auto-title entirely
options.description: Conversations stay 'Untitled' until you rename them. Drops a Phase 2 feature.

content.subject: Presets CRUD (storage + modal UI)
content.description: presets table CRUD in storage. Small modal: list / create from current conv / apply to current conv / new conv from preset / delete. No seeding.

content.subject: Pin conversation
content.description: Toggle in sidebar context menu. Pinned conversations sort to top (use idx_conversations_pinned_updated). Visual marker in sidebar list.

content.subject: Draft persistence
content.description: Save input text to conversations.draft on debounce when changed. Restore on conv load. Clear when message is sent.

content.subject: Auto-title after first response
content.description: When first assistant response completes in an untitled conv, fire one-off non-streaming completion to current model: "Title this conversation in <=6 words: ..." Update conversations.title. Same model as conv.

content.subject: Image drag-drop attachments
content.description: Drop image files (png/jpg/webp/gif) onto input area. Encode as data URL, stage as ContentPart::ImageUrl pending parts to attach to next sent message. Show thumbnails above input with remove buttons. Send-only — no in-message rendering.

content.subject: Text file drag-drop (inline as fenced code)
content.description: Drop text files (md/rs/py/txt/json/etc) up to ~256KB. Inline into input as fenced code block with language inferred from extension.

content.subject: Tests: ContentPart serde + helpers
content.description: Round-trip pure-text and mixed text+image messages; verify wire shape matches OpenAI examples. Unit-test text_str / append_text / is_empty_content edge cases.

content.description: Per delivery standards: cargo build --release, cargo clippy -- -D warnings, cargo test, then review subagent on the diff, then open PR. PR description includes the "blow away hchat.db once" note.

content.subject: Schema v2 + schema_version table
content.description: Rewrite storage.rs init_tables to create the full v2 schema directly: schema_version table, conversations with per-conv settings columns (model, system_prompt, temperature, max_tokens, use_max_tokens, top_p, frequency_penalty, presence_penalty, stop_sequences, endpoint, pinned, draft), messages with parent_id/branch_index/created_at, presets table, indexes. No data migration. Add a tiny migrations runner gated on schema_version for future-proofing.

content.subject: ContentPart enum + Message rework
content.description: Replace Message.content: String with Vec<ContentPart>. Add Text/ImageUrl variants matching OpenAI wire format. Add helpers: text(), text_str(), append_text(), is_empty_content(), images(). Verify api.rs serialization still produces a valid OpenAI request.

content.subject: Migrate ~30 content call sites in app.rs
content.description: Update every msg.content.push_str / .len / .clone / .is_empty etc. site to use the new helpers. Compile-clean, no behavior change.

content.subject: Storage CRUD for content parts + new conv columns
content.description: save_messages writes JSON-serialized Vec<ContentPart>. load_messages parses it. Add per-conversation settings load/save (model, system_prompt, temp, etc., endpoint, pinned, draft). New conversation creation stores the initial settings snapshot.
content.activeForm: Wiring storage for parts and per-conv settings

content.subject: Per-conversation settings model in ChatApp
content.description: Introduce ConversationSettings struct held in ChatApp; populated from conv on load, defaults from config on new. Settings panel mutates this struct (not the globals). Persist on change.

content.subject: Presets CRUD (storage + modal UI)
content.description: presets table CRUD in storage. Small modal: list / create from current conv / apply to current conv / new conv from preset / delete. No seeding.

content.subject: Pin conversation
content.description: Toggle in sidebar context menu. Pinned conversations sort to top (use idx_conversations_pinned_updated). Visual marker in sidebar list.

content.subject: Draft persistence
content.description: Save input text to conversations.draft on debounce when changed. Restore on conv load. Clear when message is sent.

content.subject: Auto-title after first response
content.description: When first assistant response completes in an untitled conv, fire one-off non-streaming completion to current model: "Title this conversation in <=6 words: ..." Update conversations.title. Same model as conv.

content.subject: Image drag-drop attachments
content.description: Drop image files (png/jpg/webp/gif) onto input area. Encode as data URL, stage as ContentPart::ImageUrl pending parts to attach to next sent message. Show thumbnails above input with remove buttons. Send-only — no in-message rendering.

content.subject: Text file drag-drop (inline as fenced code)
content.description: Drop text files (md/rs/py/txt/json/etc) up to ~256KB. Inline into input as fenced code block with language inferred from extension.

content.subject: Tests: ContentPart serde + helpers
content.description: Round-trip pure-text and mixed text+image messages; verify wire shape matches OpenAI examples. Unit-test text_str / append_text / is_empty_content edge cases.

content.subject: Build, clippy -D warnings, review subagent, PR
content.description: Per delivery standards: cargo build --release, cargo clippy -- -D warnings, cargo test, then review subagent on the diff, then open PR. PR description includes the "blow away hchat.db once" note.

/// MIME type for an image file extension, or None if not a supported image.
/// Limited to the formats vision-capable APIs accept (OpenAI, OpenRouter,
/// Anthropic-via-proxy). HEIC etc. would need decoding.
fn image_mime_for_ext(ext: &str) -> Option<&'static str> {
    match ext {
        "png" => Some("image/png"),
        "jpg" | "jpeg" => Some("image/jpeg"),
        "webp" => Some("image/webp"),
        "gif" => Some("image/gif"),
        _ => None,
    }
}

/// excluded so we don't dump random bytes into the chat.

input.prompt:
Code review the in-progress changes on branch `phase-2-3-storage-multimodal` of the Rust project at `/Users/heath/code/hChat`. The work bundles two phases of a chat-app refactor:

- Phase 2: storage migration (new SQLite schema with `schema_version`, per-conversation settings columns on `conversations`, new `presets` table, pre-staged branching columns on `messages`).
- Phase 3: multimodal — `Message.content` changes from `String` to `Vec<ContentPart>` (Text + ImageUrl variants matching OpenAI's wire format), plus drag-drop image attachments and drag-drop text files inlined as fenced code.

Plus delivered alongside: pinned conversations, draft persistence, auto-title via one-shot completion, presets CRUD modal.

What I want you to do:
1. Read the diff: `git diff main...HEAD` (or `git diff main` if HEAD has uncommitted work — `git status` first to know).
2. Read enough surrounding code to understand context for non-trivial changes (especially in `src/app.rs` and `src/storage.rs`).
3. Run `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` and `cargo test`. If either fails, report what failed.
4. Look hard for these specific risk areas:
   - **Concurrency / borrow issues**: there are several places where I drain a channel into a Vec first to release a `&mut self` borrow before doing storage operations. Verify those patterns are correct and not papering over a real bug.
   - **Schema correctness**: the `init_tables` SQL in `src/storage.rs` is a single CREATE block with `INSERT OR IGNORE INTO schema_version VALUES (2)` at the end. Heath said no data migration is needed (single user). But verify: a fresh DB lands at v2 cleanly, and the schema doesn't have nullable-vs-NOT-NULL inconsistencies that would break inserts.
   - **JSON content storage**: `messages.content` now stores JSON-serialized `Vec<ContentPart>`. Search() does a two-phase LIKE-then-Rust-filter to avoid false positives on JSON keys. Verify the post-filter logic is sound and doesn't drop legitimate matches.
   - **Endpoint switching on conv load**: `apply_settings` in `src/app.rs` switches `self.base_url` and re-fetches models if the loaded conv has a different stored endpoint. The model name is restored via `pending_model_after_models_load` in the `ModelsLoaded` handler. Check that race / edge cases don't leave the wrong model selected.
   - **Settings dirty-check**: `persist_settings_if_dirty` runs every frame and uses a hash signature to avoid wasted writes. Check that `apply_settings` correctly resets the signature so a freshly-loaded conv doesn't immediately re-persist what was just read.
   - **Auto-title one-shot guarantee**: the `auto_titled` column gates this. Verify that `request_auto_title` will fire only once per conv (even on transient API errors — failures should still mark the conv as auto-titled to prevent retries).
   - **Drop handling**: `handle_dropped_files` in `src/app.rs` reads files, base64-encodes images, inlines text files. Check size caps, UTF-8 handling, extension matching.
   - **`send_message` with attachments**: now allows empty text if attachments exist; verify slash-command parsing only runs when text is non-empty.
   - **`base64` crate usage**: dependency was just added (`base64 = "0.22"`). Verify the `Engine` import and standard-engine usage is the current API.
5. Note any clippy-acceptable but human-questionable code patterns (e.g. very long functions, repeated logic, naming inconsistencies).

Be specific. For every finding, cite `file:line` and explain *why* it's a concern. Don't list nits unless they're material. Keep the report under 800 words. End with a single-sentence verdict: ship-ready, ship-with-followups, or needs-fixes-before-shipping.

content.text:
`Path::new(".env").extension()` returns `None` (because `.env` is treated as a filename, not an extension). So `.env` files would land in the "unsupported file type" toast — actually safe! Nice. But files like `secrets.env` would have ext "env" and DO match `is_text_extension`. So drag-dropping a `secrets.env` file inlines the contents into the chat. That's a real footgun.

I have enough now. Let me write the report.

---

## Code Review: phase-2-3-storage-multimodal

**Build status:** clippy clean (`-D warnings`), all 37 tests pass.

### Material findings

**1. Loading a conv silently bumps its `updated_at` and reorders the sidebar.**
`src/app.rs:443` — `apply_settings` computes `settings_signature` at the end. When the conv stored an endpoint different from `self.base_url`, `apply_settings` triggers `fetch_models()` and stashes the model name in `pending_model_after_models_load`. The signature is computed against the snapshot's *current* (stale) `selected_model`. When ModelsLoaded later restores the right index (`src/app.rs:990–997`), the signature is now stale, so the next frame's `persist_settings_if_dirty` writes the same settings back — but `save_conversation_settings` does `SET updated_at = datetime('now')` (`src/storage.rs:454`), demoting/promoting the conv. Fix: after the ModelsLoaded handler updates `self.selected_model`, recompute `self.settings_signature` from the fresh snapshot, OR have `save_conversation_settings` skip the `updated_at` bump.

**2. Auto-title can fire multiple times, wasting API calls.**
`src/app.rs:471–514` — `request_auto_title` sets `self.title_rx = Some(rx)`, dropping any prior receiver. Because `mark_auto_titled` only runs when a response is *received*, sending a second user turn before the title call returns triggers a *second* `request_auto_title` (since `needs_auto_title` is still true), orphaning the first task. Not user-visible, but wastes one API call per send-before-title-returns. Either guard with an in-flight flag (`title_in_flight: bool`), or `mark_auto_titled` immediately on dispatch and revert if the result is unusable.

**3. Pending model selection silently fails when the model is missing from the new endpoint.**
`src/app.rs:990–997` — `pending_model_after_models_load.take()` consumes the name, then if `position()` returns None, falls through to the clamp branch with no toast/error. A user loading a conv whose stored model was renamed/removed gets a different model with no indication. Suggest a toast like "Model `X` no longer available, using `Y`".

**4. `.env` file inlining is a secrets footgun.**
`src/app.rs:1052` — `is_text_extension` includes `"env"`. A bare `.env` file has no extension (Path semantics) so it's safely rejected, but `secrets.env`, `prod.env`, etc. match and get inlined in a fenced block. Recommend dropping `"env"` from the allowlist or explicitly warning before inlining.

**5. Editing a message silently drops image attachments.**
`src/app.rs:936` — `edit_and_resend` does `self.messages[index].content = vec![ContentPart::Text { ... }]`, replacing any image parts. For a multimodal user message this is data loss with no warning. Either preserve non-text parts when rebuilding, or disable edit on messages with images.

**6. No upgrade path from a v1 DB.**
`src/storage.rs:67–110` — `init_tables` uses `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS conversations (...)` with the new columns inline. On an existing v1 install the CREATE is a no-op and all the new columns (`pinned`, `model`, `auto_titled`, etc.) are missing. Subsequent inserts that reference them will fail. Heath flagged this as acceptable (single-user, wipe DB). Worth adding an `ALTER TABLE` block keyed on `schema_version() < 2` for safety, or at minimum a startup error message rather than a silent column-missing failure mode.

### Verified-OK risk areas

- **Drain-into-Vec borrow patterns** (`src/app.rs:953–977` for title_rx, `1018+` for stream rx): pattern is correct — `&mut self.title_rx` is released at the end of the match arm before storage calls.
- **Schema correctness on fresh DB**: `schema_is_at_v2_after_init` test confirms; nullable columns in `messages` (`parent_id`, `created_at`) and `conversations` (most settings) are consistent with insert sites.
- **JSON content search**: post-filter logic in `src/storage.rs:284–342` is sound. `search_post_filters_json_keys` test verifies "image_url" keyword doesn't false-match. Lower-case comparison is consistent.
- **base64 0.22 API**: `base64::engine::general_purpose::STANDARD.encode(&bytes)` with `use base64::Engine as _;` is correct for 0.22 (`src/app.rs:524–525`).
- **Settings dirty-check reset on load**: `apply_settings` resets `settings_signature` (modulo finding 1).
- **Drop handling**: size caps (8MB image, 256KB text), UTF-8 check via `String::from_utf8`, extension match all look right.
- **Slash command + attachments**: `src/app.rs:570` correctly gates command parsing on `!raw.is_empty()`, so attachment-only sends bypass parsing.

### Nits worth a follow-up

- `src/storage.rs:130 fn schema_version` is `#[allow(dead_code)]` — only used in tests. Either delete or wire to a startup migration check.
- `src/app.rs:438` `PresetAction` matching `Apply | NewFrom | Delete` then re-matching is awkward — the inner `match act` redoes work the outer match could have done in one pass.
- `pending_attachments` filename is dropped at send time — not searchable later. Consider stashing it as a small text part.

### Verdict

**Ship-with-followups** — no blocking bugs, all tests/clippy green, but findings #1 (sidebar reorder on load) and #5 (image loss on edit) are user-visible enough to fix soon, and #4 (`.env` inlining) deserves a one-line patch before this lands in any users' hands.

content.subject: Schema v2 + schema_version table
content.description: Rewrite storage.rs init_tables to create the full v2 schema directly: schema_version table, conversations with per-conv settings columns (model, system_prompt, temperature, max_tokens, use_max_tokens, top_p, frequency_penalty, presence_penalty, stop_sequences, endpoint, pinned, draft), messages with parent_id/branch_index/created_at, presets table, indexes. No data migration. Add a tiny migrations runner gated on schema_version for future-proofing.

content.subject: ContentPart enum + Message rework
content.description: Replace Message.content: String with Vec<ContentPart>. Add Text/ImageUrl variants matching OpenAI wire format. Add helpers: text(), text_str(), append_text(), is_empty_content(), images(). Verify api.rs serialization still produces a valid OpenAI request.

content.subject: Migrate ~30 content call sites in app.rs
content.description: Update every msg.content.push_str / .len / .clone / .is_empty etc. site to use the new helpers. Compile-clean, no behavior change.

content.subject: Storage CRUD for content parts + new conv columns
content.description: save_messages writes JSON-serialized Vec<ContentPart>. load_messages parses it. Add per-conversation settings load/save (model, system_prompt, temp, etc., endpoint, pinned, draft). New conversation creation stores the initial settings snapshot.
content.activeForm: Wiring storage for parts and per-conv settings

content.subject: Per-conversation settings model in ChatApp
content.description: Introduce ConversationSettings struct held in ChatApp; populated from conv on load, defaults from config on new. Settings panel mutates this struct (not the globals). Persist on change.

content.subject: Presets CRUD (storage + modal UI)
content.description: presets table CRUD in storage. Small modal: list / create from current conv / apply to current conv / new conv from preset / delete. No seeding.

content.subject: Pin conversation
content.description: Toggle in sidebar context menu. Pinned conversations sort to top (use idx_conversations_pinned_updated). Visual marker in sidebar list.

content.subject: Draft persistence
content.description: Save input text to conversations.draft on debounce when changed. Restore on conv load. Clear when message is sent.

content.subject: Auto-title after first response
content.description: When first assistant response completes in an untitled conv, fire one-off non-streaming completion to current model: "Title this conversation in <=6 words: ..." Update conversations.title. Same model as conv.

content.subject: Image drag-drop attachments
content.description: Drop image files (png/jpg/webp/gif) onto input area. Encode as data URL, stage as ContentPart::ImageUrl pending parts to attach to next sent message. Show thumbnails above input with remove buttons. Send-only — no in-message rendering.

content.subject: Text file drag-drop (inline as fenced code)
content.description: Drop text files (md/rs/py/txt/json/etc) up to ~256KB. Inline into input as fenced code block with language inferred from extension.

content.subject: Tests: ContentPart serde + helpers
content.description: Round-trip pure-text and mixed text+image messages; verify wire shape matches OpenAI examples. Unit-test text_str / append_text / is_empty_content edge cases.

content.subject: Build, clippy -D warnings, review subagent, PR
content.description: Per delivery standards: cargo build --release, cargo clippy -- -D warnings, cargo test, then review subagent on the diff, then open PR. PR description includes the "blow away hchat.db once" note.

Phase 2+3: storage migration + multimodal (per-conv settings, presets, pin, drafts, auto-title, image+text drop)

Bundles Phase 2 (storage migration, per-conversation settings, presets,
pin, drafts, auto-title) and Phase 3 (multimodal Message content, image
and text-file drag-drop) into a single PR. Bundling avoids two user-data
migrations on the same column: messages.content stores OpenAI-shaped
JSON parts (Vec<ContentPart>) from day one rather than migrating from
TEXT later for vision support.

Schema (v2, fresh):
- schema_version table for future ALTER gating
- conversations: model, system_prompt, temperature, max_tokens,
  use_max_tokens, top_p, frequency/presence_penalty, stop_sequences,
  endpoint, pinned, draft, auto_titled
- messages: parent_id and branch_index (pre-staged for Phase 4),
  created_at, content as JSON-serialized Vec<ContentPart>
- presets table mirroring the conversation settings columns
- indexes on (conversation_id, position), (parent_id), and
  (pinned DESC, updated_at DESC)

Per-conversation settings: live ChatApp fields are the source of truth
for the active conv. Loading restores them from the row (with config
fallback for None); a per-frame hash dirty check persists changes
without a separate save button. Endpoint switches re-fetch models and
restore the stored model name when ModelsLoaded arrives, with a toast
when the model isn't available on the new endpoint.

Multimodal: ContentPart::{Text, ImageUrl} matches OpenAI's chat
completions wire shape; Ollama text-only still accepts the array form.
Helpers (text_str, append_text, is_empty_content, images) keep the
~30 existing content access sites mechanical.

Search: two-phase (SQL LIKE prune + Rust JSON-aware filter) so that
"image_url" or "text" don't false-match against JSON keys.

Drag-drop: image files (png/jpg/webp/gif up to 8MB) staged as ImageUrl
parts shown as chips; text files (rs/py/md/json/etc up to 256KB)
inlined as fenced code with language inferred from extension. .env
deliberately excluded to avoid leaking secrets. Editing a multimodal
message preserves attached images.

Auto-title: one-shot non-streaming completion against the conv's
current model after the first assistant response. Marks auto_titled
on dispatch (one fire per conv even if calls fail).

Tests (37 passing): ContentPart serde round-trip, helper edge cases,
schema versioning, message round-trip with images, search post-filter
correctness on JSON keys, per-conv settings round-trip, pinned sort,
drafts, auto-title flag, presets CRUD.

Note for the single-user upgrade path: heath confirmed no data
migration needed — wipe ~/Library/Application Support/hchat/hchat.db
once before launching the new build.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF

gh pr create --title "Phase 2+3: storage migration + multimodal content" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary

Bundles **Phase 2** (storage migration, per-conversation settings, presets, pin, drafts, auto-title) and **Phase 3** (multimodal content, image + text-file drag-drop) into a single PR.

The bundling decision: if Phase 2 had landed first with `messages.content TEXT`, Phase 3 would have needed a second user-data migration on the same column to support image parts. Doing them together lets `content` ship as JSON-serialized `Vec<ContentPart>` from day one — the same shape OpenAI's chat completions API accepts for multimodal.

### Schema (v2, fresh)

- `schema_version` table for future ALTER gating
- `conversations`: model, system_prompt, temperature, max_tokens, use_max_tokens, top_p, frequency/presence_penalty, stop_sequences, endpoint, pinned, draft, auto_titled
- `messages`: parent_id and branch_index (pre-staged for Phase 4 branching), created_at, content as JSON-serialized `Vec<ContentPart>`
- `presets` table mirroring the conversation settings columns
- Indexes: `(conversation_id, position)`, `(parent_id)`, `(pinned DESC, updated_at DESC)`

### Per-conversation settings

The live `ChatApp` fields are the source of truth for the active conv. Loading restores them from the row (with config fallback for `None`); a per-frame hash dirty-check persists changes without a separate save button. Endpoint switches re-fetch models and restore the stored model name when `ModelsLoaded` arrives, with a toast when the model isn't available on the new endpoint.

### Multimodal

`ContentPart::{Text, ImageUrl}` matches OpenAI's wire shape; Ollama text-only also accepts the array form. Helpers (`text_str`, `append_text`, `is_empty_content`, `images`) keep the ~30 existing content access sites mechanical.

Search is two-phase (SQL `LIKE` prune + Rust JSON-aware filter) so that "image_url" or "text" don't false-match against JSON keys.

### Drag-drop

- Image files (png/jpg/webp/gif up to 8MB) → staged as `ImageUrl` parts, shown as chips above the input.
- Text files (rs/py/md/json/etc up to 256KB) → inlined as fenced code with language inferred from extension.
- `.env` deliberately excluded to avoid leaking secrets.
- Editing a multimodal user message preserves attached images.

### Auto-title

One-shot non-streaming completion against the conv's current model after the first assistant response. Marks `auto_titled` on dispatch so transient API failures don't trigger retries on every send.

## Heads-up: wipe the local DB once

This is a fresh v2 schema with no migration from v1 (you confirmed: single user, no migration needed). Before launching the new build:

```bash
rm -f ~/Library/Application\ Support/hchat/hchat.db*
```

(or the equivalent path on Linux: `~/.local/share/hchat/hchat.db*`)

## Verification

- `cargo build --release` — clean
- `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` — clean
- `cargo test` — 37 passing (ContentPart serde, helper edges, schema versioning, message round-trip with images, search post-filter on JSON keys, per-conv settings, pinned sort, drafts, auto-title flag, presets CRUD)
- Code-review subagent pass: 5 review-driven fixes applied (sidebar reorder on load, auto-title duplicate-fire guard, missing-model toast, `.env` exclusion, image preservation on edit)

## Test plan

- [ ] Wipe local DB, launch, verify fresh-start works
- [ ] Send a message; reload app; verify it persists
- [ ] Change temperature/system prompt mid-conv; reload; verify per-conv settings restored
- [ ] Save a preset, apply it to a different conv, "New chat from preset"
- [ ] Pin a conversation; verify it sorts to top after a new conv is created
- [ ] Type into the input, switch conversations, switch back; verify draft restored
- [ ] First response in a fresh conv triggers an auto-title; rename manually; verify next response doesn't re-title
- [ ] Drag a PNG onto the window; chip appears; send a vision-capable model query
- [ ] Drag a `.rs` file; verify it inlines as a fenced ```rust block
- [ ] Drag a `.env` file; verify it's rejected with a toast
- [ ] Switch endpoints; verify a conv made against OpenRouter doesn't silently route to Ollama

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -5
